content.text:
```mdl
@ Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement (CNDA)
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_10_nda_7

open std.collections

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties (the two contracting entities)
type Party = { name: string }

@ Confidential information as in Article 2
type ConfidentialInfo = {
    id: int,
    description: string,
    is_marked_confidential: bool
}

@ A Representative is a person or entity that the Receiving Party may
  disclose Confidential Information to (Article 2.05)
type Representative = {
    party: Party,
    need_to_know: bool
}

@ A Disclosure event records that a piece of Confidential Information is
  sent to a recipient (used to formulate the deontic rules)
type Disclosure = {
    recipient: Party,
    info: ConfidentialInfo
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the concrete objects that the solver will assign values to
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The party that receives the Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”)
entity receiving_party: Party

@ The party that provides the Confidential Information (the “Disclosing Party”)
entity disclosing_party: Party

@ The piece of Confidential Information that is the subject of the agreement
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInfo

@ The person or entity that is authorized to receive the Confidential Information
entity representative: Representative

@ The actual act of disclosing Confidential Information (used in the rules)
entity disclosure: Disclosure

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rules – normative statements (deontic modalities) derived from the CNDA
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Article 2.03 – Obligation to keep the information in confidence
rule O keep_confidentiality:
    true always

@ Article 2.05 – Prohibition of disclosure to anyone who is **not** an
  authorized Representative
rule F unauthorized_disclosure:
    disclosure.recipient = representative.party always

@ Article 2.06 – Exception for disclosures required by law (permission)
rule P legal_disclosure:
    true always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Hypothesis to The Receiving Party may share Confidential Information with
# third‑parties such as consultants, agents or professional advisors.
# This is modeled as a permission that directly conflicts with the
# prohibition above, allowing a solver to detect the contradiction.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Hypothesis – Permission to share with third‑parties
rule P share_with_third_party:
    true always
```
